user_query:
I run a sports bar that screens Western Sydney Wanderers games. Please set up a small, self-contained workflow that generates pre-game action items for upcoming screenings and provides a daily scheduling template.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/fixtures.csv
- input/staff.csv
- config/tasks.yaml

What I need:
1) Modify configuration:
   - Append these two tasks to config/tasks.yaml under pre_game_tasks so they are part of the generated notes:
     - minutes_before: 90, task: "Print drinks special flyers (Wanderers matchday) and distribute", role: "Bar Lead"
     - minutes_before: 45, task: "Warm up beer garden projector and outdoor speakers", role: "AV Tech"
   (Do not remove any existing tasks.)

2) Implement and run a generator script:
   - Create a Python 3 CLI script at tools/generate_pre_game_notes.py that:
     - Accepts: --fixtures input/fixtures.csv, --staff input/staff.csv, --config config/tasks.yaml, --out out/action_items, --now <ISO8601>
     - Reads fixtures.csv (ISO 8601 kickoff with timezone) and selects all matches within the next 7 days from the supplied --now.
     - Reads staff.csv to map roles to staff names; if a role listed in tasks has no matching staff, use "TBD".
     - Reads config/tasks.yaml and compiles a pre-game timeline by subtracting each task’s minutes_before from the kickoff timestamp.
     - Generates one Markdown file per selected match at out/action_items with this exact filename pattern:
       - out/action_items/WSW_<YYYY-MM-DD>_vs_<Opponent>_pre_game.md (opponent taken exactly from fixtures.csv, spaces kept)
     - Each Markdown file must include at minimum:
       - A title line: "Pre-game action items: Western Sydney Wanderers vs <Opponent>"
       - A line: "Kickoff: <kickoff_local_iso from fixtures.csv>"
       - An "Assigned staff" section listing each role from staff.csv and the assigned name.
       - A "Timeline" section sorted by scheduled time ascending. Each line must show the absolute scheduled time in ISO 8601 (same offset as kickoff), the task text, and the assignee name in parentheses. Example line format (not strict on punctuation beyond these fields):
         - "2026-04-20T18:15:00+10:00 - Print drinks special flyers (Bar Lead: Marco)"
       - Ensure the two new tasks you added to config appear in this timeline with correctly computed times.

   - Run the script once to produce concrete outputs for verification using:
     python3 tools/generate_pre_game_notes.py \
       --fixtures input/fixtures.csv \
       --staff input/staff.csv \
       --config config/tasks.yaml \
       --out out/action_items \
       --now 2026-04-19T12:00:00+10:00

   - For this run, generate notes for all matches within the next 7 days of the supplied --now. Based on the provided fixtures, that should produce exactly these two files:
     - out/action_items/WSW_2026-04-20_vs_Macarthur FC_pre_game.md
     - out/action_items/WSW_2026-04-24_vs_Newcastle Jets_pre_game.md

3) Add a scheduling template:
   - Create out/cron/cron.txt containing exactly one crontab line that runs the generator daily at 09:00 local time to refresh the next 7 days of notes. The line must start with "0 9 * * *" and invoke the script using the same relative paths as above, passing --now $(date -Iseconds). It’s okay if this is a template (i.e., it may assume it runs from the repository root), but the single line must be a valid crontab entry.

Notes:
- Do not rely on any external services; everything should run from the provided files.
- The two new tasks must be added to config/tasks.yaml and reflected in both generated Markdown files.
- Keep all outputs under out/ as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
